articles.title: Dollar Declines Amid War-End Optimism
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Wednesday added to Tuesday's losses and fell by -0.34%. The dollar is under pressure due to optimism that the war in Iran will end soon. Wednesday's stock rally also reduced liquidity demand for the dollar. The dollar recovered from its worst level after better-than-expected...

articles.title: Dollar Falls on Optimism Iran War Will End Soon
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) today is adding to Tuesday's losses and is down by -0.51%. The dollar is under pressure due to optimism that the war in Iran will end soon. Today's stock rally has also reduced liquidity demand for the dollar. The dollar recovered from its worst level...

articles.title: US 10-year yield steadies as strong data backs steady Fed rate outlook
articles.expand: US Treasury yields recover across the curve on Wednesday, with the 10-year Treasury note erasing earlier losses after strong US economic data increased the odds of keeping interest rates unchanged throughout the year.

articles.title: EUR/USD edges higher as easing US-Iran tensions outweigh support from strong US data
articles.expand: EUR/USD extends its advance for a second consecutive day on Wednesday, climbing to one-week highs as improving optimism around the US-Iran war lifts risk sentiment, pushing the Euro (EUR) higher and weighing on the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Euro comes out swinging: Can the "Trump Reversal" sustain EUR/USD's upside bias?
articles.expand: EUR/USD has surged towards 1.1600 due to "war optimism" following US President Donald Trump's comments and the hawkish stance of the ECB, which has created a yield differential favoring the Euro. The "Trump Reversal" seems to hold the key.

articles.title: Ottawa extending 2% alcohol tax hike cap for another two years

articles.title: Fuel crisis powers surge in EV interest in Asia
articles.expand: Motorists across the Asia-Pacific region are switching to electric vehicles at a rapid pace, as rising fuel costs due to the Middle East war force consumers and companies ​to reconsider their reliance on petrol and diesel vehicles.

articles.title: Is risk-on, back on? The AUDUSD is higher but it is not running quickly to the upside.
articles.expand: The AUDUSD typically trades as a classic risk-on/risk-off barometer. When equities are under pressure and yields are pushing higher, the pair tends to drift lower as traders reduce risk exposure. Conversely, when stocks firm and yields ease, AUDUSD often benefits from a more constructive, risk-on backdrop. Today, the pair is higher by about 0.62%, ...

articles.expand: Canada’s economy expanded by 0.1% mom in January, beating expectations of a flat reading, as strength in resource-related sectors helped offset ongoing weakness in manufacturing. articles.expand: The BOJ’s March summary shows a board in crisis: hike rates to defend the yen, or stay low and watch it burn?
